ogTitle: Deans Marsh vs Deddick Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Deans Marsh and Deddick Valley.
heading: Deans Marsh vs Deddick Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Deans Marsh, Victoria and Deddick Valley, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Deans Marsh has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Deddick Valley (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Deans Marsh or Deddick Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Deans Marsh has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Deddick Valley scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Deans Marsh scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Deddick Valley scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Deans Marsh or Deddick Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Deans Marsh has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
